global-furniture-rules-desc: Set global rules here to automatically apply to all furniture with these attributes
home.subtitle: Rapid Modding Tool for Mewgenics
home.project-notice-p1: This project is a rapid editing tool developed by analyzing existing mods from Nexus Mods and combined with unpacked game data.
home.project-notice-p2: Note: Features have not been fully tested. Some modifications may not take effect in-game; this is normal as many fields in .gon files remain unexplored.
home.import-notice-p1: Exported ZIP packages double as configuration files. You can re-import them to restore your settings and continue your work.
mutations.part.head: Head
npc.help-basic-desc: This configuration determines the number of cats required to gift to NPCs to unlock their reward tiers.
npc.help-fixed-desc: If you want to significantly simplify the unlocking process, select Fixed (SET) and set the value to 1. This means only 1 cat is needed per tier, regardless of the original requirement.
npc.help-mult-desc: Select Multiplier (MULT). For example, if you want to reduce the requirement by 80%, set the multiplier to 0.2.Formula: New Requirement = Original × 0.2 (rounded down).
help.char.basic-desc: Selecting passives will automatically copy and merge all preset attributes (e.g., Strength +1, Speed +2) into the character's initial stats.
help.char.advanced-desc: Advanced mode allows you to directly edit underlying logic fields. A 'passive skill' in the game engine is composed of many tiny logic entries. By manually entering paths, you can achieve more refined customization.
help.char.examples-title: Common Advanced Entry Examples (Path, value 1 means enabled)
help.char.example-4: Collect all drops at battle end
help.char.example-5: No injury when cat is downed
help.class.intro: This section is for modifying initial class stat modifiers and the weight of stat growth per level (Pool).
help.class.passive-desc: Class innate passives follow the same logic as character passives: you can select preset skills or inject specific logic fields via advanced mode below.
help.setbonus.intro: This modifies [Set Bonuses], which are extra rewards gained when wearing 2/3/4 pieces of the same equipment set.
help.setbonus.warning: Note: This does not change the stats of hats, weapons, or other equipment themselves; it only modifies the bonus values triggered by set combinations.
help.mutation.intro: This tool allows for simultaneous adjustment of stat bonuses provided by all body parts (ears, tails, skins, etc.).
help.mutation.quick-desc: Use 'Quick Apply' to rapidly enhance all mutation stats. For example, setting the multiplier to 2.0 will double the benefits from all mutations in-game.
help.mutation.non-neg-desc: 'Only process non-negative IDs' is typically to protect game logic, avoiding modifications to special negative index entries used for deducting stats.
class.advanced.title: Advanced Mode
class.advanced.hint: Modifications here will be directly written into the 'passives' structure of innate_passives. It works in parallel with 'Select Passive Override' above and can be used simultaneously (they will be merged).
class.advanced.empty: No fields added yet. You can search for paths above and add them.
class.passive-override-title: Select Passive Override (Extract and merge passives from different levels)
